title: axprobe scenario
description: A test scenario for axprobe, authored under .axprobe/<scenario>.yaml. Only the scenario (intent/goal, checks, scenario-specific credentials) lives here; the box/install is inherited from the sibling .axprobe/config.yaml unless the scenario defines its own box. Public, versioned interface.

schema_version.description: Manifest contract version. Currently "1". Bumped on breaking changes.

name.description: Scenario name. Used for reports and as the credential-store namespace.

intent.description: Plain-language description of the scenario. The human-authored source; `explore` derives goal/checks/credentials from it.

goal.description: What the LLM driver should pursue (Layer 1). Often derived from intent.

stop_when.description: Optional natural-language stop condition for the driver.

success_check.description: Command/assertion describing success. Informational for now.

box.description: Optional. The environment + install. Normally inherited from .axprobe/config.yaml; set here only for a self-contained scenario.

image.description: Container image for the box, e.g. ubuntu:24.04.

setup.description: Commands to install the tool under test. A failing step aborts the run.

probes.description: Layer 0 scripted commands, run when no --model is given (stand-in for the LLM driver).

expect.description: The AX bar (PM-owned definition of done). When set, the run exits non-zero if the result misses it — the CI gate / TDD signal. Counts are upper bounds (<=).

reset.description: Return the fixture to a clean baseline BEFORE the run, so a re-run starts from scratch. The box is disposable (in-box files reset for free); reset covers cross-run state.

secrets.description: Purge this scenario's cached credentials (Keychain) so an auth fixture runs cold instead of restoring a token.

paths.description: Clear the CONTENTS of these box directories (keep the dirs) — e.g. a mounted/persistent project folder where init ran.

credentials.description: Credentials the secret broker may collect, store and inject when the driver gates.

items.description: A human-gated requirement. `kind` selects which broker resolver runs when the driver gates. file/value are implemented; oauth is approved (resolver pending).

name.description: Credential id, used for storage and gate matching.

kind.description: file: a file path the user provides (uses inject). value: a literal secret value (uses inject). oauth: a browser login the harness runs (uses login_command + callback_port).

prompt.description: file/value: shown to the user when collecting the credential.

inject.description: file/value only: where the credential lands inside the box. Use exactly one field.

box_path.description: kind:file → write the file here inside the box.

env.description: kind:value → export under this env var for login shells in the box.

mode.description: kind:oauth → device (default; no callback — the broker streams the login URL+code to the host and waits) or loopback (uses callback_port).

login_command.description: kind:oauth → the command the broker runs in the box to start the browser login.

callback_port.description: kind:oauth, mode:loopback only → the loopback port the callback uses; forwarded host↔box so the browser redirect reaches the CLI in the box.
